label: root isogloss
definition: Uniquely shared roots attested in Celtic and Germanic with dissimilar suffixes or nonidentical ablaut patterns.

label: morphological isogloss
definition: Uniquely shared formations whose roots are identifiable through cognates in at least one other Indo-European branch.

label: semantic isogloss
definition: Formations found in at least one other Indo-European branch, but with a uniquely shared meaning in Celtic and Germanic.

label: lexical isogloss
definition: Uniquely shared but otherwise etymologically isolated lexemes with no demonstrable derivational structure.

label: Indo-Europeanism
definition: Etymologically nonisolated, but derivationally isolated isogloss.

label: possible Indo-Europeanism / semantic isolation
definition: Derivationally nonisolated, but semantically isolated isogloss.

label: uncertain Indo-Europeanism
definition: Etymologically isolated isogloss that does not violate PIE phonotactics.

label: undefined loan
definition: Loanword that may belong to a more specific loan category but cannot be assigned securely.

label: third-party loan
definition: Loanword from an unknown source, e.g. substrate word or Wanderwort.

label: mutual loan
definition: Loanword originating in Celtic or Germanic where direction of borrowing cannot be established.

label: Celtic-to-Germanic loan
definition: Loanword direction interpreted as Celtic to Germanic.

label: Germanic-to-Celtic loan
definition: Loanword direction interpreted as Germanic to Celtic.
